---
title: "Wildfires"
subtitle: "Trends in Portugal"
author: "Daytonna Anderson"
institute: "University of Illinois at Chicago"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  xaringan::moon_reader:
    css: xaringan-themer.css
    lib_dir: libs
    nature:
      ratio: "16:9"
      highlightStyle: github
      highlightLines: true
      countIncrementalSlides: false
---

```{r load-packages, include = FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(tidymodels)
library(palmerpenguins)
library(knitr)
library(xaringan)
library(xaringanthemer)
library(janitor)
library(broom)
library(DATAstudio)
```

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(fig.retina = 3, dpi = 300, fig.width = 6, fig.asp = 0.618, out.width = "80%")
```


```{r load-data, include=FALSE}
wildfire <- read_csv("data/wildfire.csv")
```

```{r glimpse-data, eval = T, include = FALSE}
data("wildfire")

wildfire <- wildfire %>%
  clean_names()
```

```{r load-picture, include=FALSE}
style_xaringan(
  title_slide_background_image = "img/portugal-wildfire.jpg"
)
```


class: center, middle

# Overall Goal/ Research Question


How do environmental conditions influence the daily total burned area at the national scale?

---

class: inverse, middle, center

background-image: url("img/portugal-wildfire-2.jpg")
background-size: cover

# Data

---

# Variables

| Column | Meaning |
|---|---|
| Burnt Area (`burnt_area`) | Daily burnt area in hectares |
| Fire Weather Index (`fwi`) | Numeric rating of fire intensity |
| Buildup Index (`bui`) | Numeric rating of fuel available for combustion |
| Initial Spread Index (`isi`) | Numeric rating of expected rate of fire spread |
| Fine Fuel Moisture Code (`ffmc`) | Numeric rating of moisture content of litter and other fuels |
| Daily Severity Rating (`dsr`) | Numeric rating of difficulty to control fires |
| Duff Moisture Code (`dmc`) | Numeric rating of average moisture content of lose compact organic layers |
| Drought Code (`dc`) | Rating of average moisture content of compact organic layers |
|`day`, `month`, `year` | Time stamp for each point |

---

## Variables Used

- Response Variable: `burnt_area`
- Predictor Variable(s): `fwi`, `isi`, `bui`, `dc`


## Comparison Groups

- High vs. low fire risk days (`fwi`)
- High vs. low fuel availability (`bui`)
- Seasonal comparisons (`month`)


## Hypothesis


- $H_0$: Burnt area has no significant relationship with FWI, ISI, BUI, and DC

- $H_A$: Burnt area has a significant relationship with at least one of FWI, ISI, BUI, and DC

---

class: inverse, middle, center

background-image: url("img/portugal-wildfire-2.jpg")
background-size: cover

# Initial Data Exploration

---

# Total Burn Days

.pull-left[
```{r summary-data, eval = F}
wildfire %>%
  summarize(
    total_days = n(),
    zero_burn_days = sum(burnt_area == 0, 
                         na.rm = TRUE),
   nonzero_burn_days = sum(burnt_area > 0, 
                           na.rm = TRUE)
  )
```
]

.pull-right[
```{r burnt-distribution, echo = F, eval = T, out.width="100%"}
#| warning: false
ggplot(wildfire, aes(x = burnt_area)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 25) +
  scale_y_log10() +
  labs(
    title = "Daily Burned Area",
    x = "Burned Area (hectares per day)",
    y = "Count (log)"
  )
```

```{r log-data, echo = F, eval = T}
wildfire <- wildfire %>%
  mutate(log_burn = log10(burnt_area +1))
```
]
---

class: center, middle

.pull-left[
```{r burnt-vs-fwi, echo = F, eval = T, message = F}
ggplot(wildfire, aes(x = fwi, y = log_burn)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm") +
  labs(
    title = "Burned Area vs Fire Weather Index",
    x = "Fire Weather Index (FWI)",
    y = "Burned Area (log)"
  )
```

```{r burnt-vs-isi, echo = F, eval = T, message = F}
ggplot(wildfire, aes(x = isi, y = log_burn)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm") +
  labs(
    title = "Burned Area vs Fire Weather Index",
    x = "Initial Spread Index (ISI)",
    y = "Burned Area (log)"
  )
```
]

.pull-right[
```{r burnt-vs-bui, echo = F, eval = T, message = F}
ggplot(wildfire, aes(x = bui, y = log_burn)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm") +
  labs(
    title = "Burned Area vs Fire Weather Index",
    x = "Buildup Index (BUI)",
    y = "Burned Area (log)"
  )
```

```{r burnt-vs-dc, echo = F, eval = T, message = F}
ggplot(wildfire, aes(x = dc, y = log_burn)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm") +
  labs(
    title = "Burned Area vs Fire Weather Index",
    x = "Drought Code (DC)",
    y = "Burned Area (log)"
  )
```
]

---

class: inverse, middle, center

background-image: url("img/portugal-wildfire-2.jpg")
background-size: cover

# Exploratory Variable Testing

---

class: center, middle

```{r seasonal-fires, echo = F, eval = T}
ggplot(wildfire, aes(x = factor(month), y = log_burn)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(
    title = "Seasonal Variation in Burned Area",
    x = "Month",
    y = "Burned Area (log)"
  )
```

---

class: center, middle

## Do high-risk days actually burn more area?

```{r fwi-high-low, echo = F, eval = T}
wildfire <- wildfire %>%
  mutate(
    fwi_group = if_else(fwi > median(fwi, na.rm = TRUE),
                        "High FWI", "Low FWI")
  )
```

```{r fwi-high-low-plot, echo = F, eval = T}
ggplot(wildfire, aes(x = fwi_group, y = log_burn)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(
    title = "Burned Area by Fire Weather Risk Level",
    x = "FWI Group",
    y = "Log Burned Area"
  )
```

---

class: center, middle

## Does more available fuel equal larger fires?

```{r bui-high-low, echo = F, eval = T}
wildfire <- wildfire %>%
  mutate(
    bui_group = if_else(bui > median(bui, na.rm = TRUE),
                        "High BUI", "Low BUI")
  )
```

```{r bui-high-low-plot, echo = F, eval = T}
ggplot(wildfire, aes(x = bui_group, y = log_burn)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(
    title = "Burned Area by Fuel Availability",
    x = "BUI Group",
    y = "Log Burned Area"
  )
```

---

class: inverse, middle, center

background-image: url("img/portugal-wildfire-2.jpg")
background-size: cover

# Statisical Testing

---

```{r mean-CI, echo = F, eval = T}
t.test(wildfire$burnt_area)
```

```{r lm-model, echo = F, eval = T}
model <- lm(log10(burnt_area +1) ~ fwi + isi + bui + dc, data = wildfire)

summary(model)
```

---

class: center, middle

```{r plotted-residuals, echo = F, eval = T}
par(mfrow = c(2,2))
plot(model)
```

---

class: center, middle

```{r residul-model, echo = F, eval = T}
wildfire <- wildfire %>%
  mutate(residuals = resid(model),
         fitted = fitted(model))
```

```{r plotting-own-residual, echo = F, eval = T}
ggplot(wildfire, aes(x = fitted, y = residuals)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(title = "Residuals vs Fitted Values")
```
